calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-24-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darrel Horsted for Hugo Delgado; Richard Canatella, objector in pro per.

The Court noted that neither side had submitted time sheets, and inquired if the parties wanted to go forward without the time sheets or continue the matter for submittal of time sheets. Mr. Canatella responded that only a small amount is at issue -- $5000 -- and he was prepared to have the Court proceed without time sheets. Mr. Horsted agreed. The Court then asked about the failure to deposit estate funds into an interest-bearing account. Mt. Horsted stated that the bank was paying minimal interest so that the amount of lost interestwas a minimal amount, and that none of the estate beneficiaries had objected to the
failure to obtain that interest. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. Reporter: David Disbrow, CSR #7768)

Verified Petition For Order For Turnover Of Estate Funds And Sanctions, Supporting Declaration Of Hugo A. Delgado And Darrel C. Horstead; Memo. Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darryl Horsted for (administrator) Hugo Delgado. Richard Canatella (via court call) in Propria Persona. Lee Bardellini (via court call) for First American Title and Nancy Chan.

Mr. Horsted reported that the former attorney placed a demand on the estate property and will not release it, that they need an order requiring a return of the funds and that they cannot close the estate until the administrator has control of those funds. Mr. Canatella reported that he filed a petition in October 2014 regarding expenses, that he carried the case for 1 year, that property was sold, that he wanted to be reimbursed for out of pocket expenses, that there was an aggressive reverse mortgage lender to deal with, that there was lots of legal work, that Mr. Horsted opposed the petition, that there were competing demands for statutory fees, that Judge Busch took the petition off calendar in January 2015 with the agreement that the claim would be handled with the final distribution and that Mr. Horsted filed a motion for sanctions which was denied. The Court asked Mr. Canatella why should it not make the order turning over the funds to the estate. Mr. Canatella stated that the title company is holding the funds and that they will turn it over. Mr. Horsted stated that Mr. Canatella has not authorized the turnover of the funds. Mr. Bardellini reported that at the close of escrow a demand on funds was made by Mr. Canatella, that they cannot release the funds until the dispute is handled, that they either need authorization from the parties or a court order, that they are trying to avoid an interpleader which would generate legal fees and that they will abide by a court order or joint request. Mr. Canatella stated that he released the claim in January 2015 subject to the claim being litigated with the final distribution and that an actual order was not issued. The verified petition for order for turnover of estate funds and sanctions, etc., was taken under submission.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (124 Newman Street S.F.)
Off Calendar Sep-04-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella for Hugo Delgado.

Mr. Canatella indicated that the issue of the Request for Special Notice was addressed. The sale was confirmed at $880,000.00 to James Maxwell (Agent) on behalf of his Client Vanessa Clare Whitlam based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). Bond is waived.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
